page: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Dpsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64-based Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for attaching external block storage.

In benchmark testing, the ARM-based processor demonstrates average single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core score of 2074.78 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1677.0. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength, reaching 33.08 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-bound workloads show limitations; the multi-core Geekbench score is 3069.0, and the Passmark memory score of 1025.96 is categorized as poor. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is moderate at 46.88 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is lower at 20.97 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2ps_v6 offers a low-cost entry point into ARM64 computing on Azure, providing dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading at a resource density suitable for lightweight workloads. Due to its weak multi-core scaling and poor performance in complex tasks like LLM inference, this instance is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight application hosting, development environments, and simple single-threaded utility tasks rather than database-heavy or high-compute applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2ps_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dpsv6 family, powered by an ARM64 Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs) running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2074.78 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 33.08 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive tasks are relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 3069.0 and a Passmark memory score of 1025.96. Qualitatively, the D2ps_v6 provides a cost-effective entry point for ARM-native development, lightweight web serving, and basic utility workloads that do not require high memory bandwidth or parallel processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering a cost-effective option for lightweight web serving and ARM development.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2ps_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 2 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-based virtual machine with dedicated physical cores and balanced memory, designed for lightweight utility workloads and cost-efficient application hosting.
bullet_points: ARM64 architecture featuring a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor
2 dedicated physical cores with 8.0 GB of system memory
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 33.08 GB/sec
No local storage included, but compatible with Premium Storage options
Best suited for lightweight web serving, ARM development, and basic utility tasks
